rc_nonzero_valid_json_success._note: R2 gh rc-quirk: gh pr checks exits non-zero even when the JSON payload is well-formed (failing/pending/no-checks case). PRE-#399 the caller's `if ci_states=$(chp_ci_status ...); then` failed → ci_states='[]' → gate rc 1 (false-negative not-green). POST-#399 the leaf inspects stdout, derives 'green' from the parseable all-SUCCESS payload → ci_is_green rc 0 (correct-green). This is the ONE documented decision change in the W1d parity contract — old=1, new=0 asserted explicitly by the parity harness.

rc_nonzero_garbage_stdout._note: Non-JSON stdout + non-zero rc = genuine transport failure; leaf rc != 0. Both OLD and NEW paths funnel this into rc 1 — parity preserved for the genuine-error case.

rc_nonzero_empty_array._note: R2 gh rc-quirk: `gh pr checks` exits non-zero when a PR has ZERO checks configured while emitting a parseable empty array on stdout. Pre-#399 caller's `if ci_states=$(chp_ci_status …)` failed on rc 1 → else-branch → ci_states='[]' → gate rc 1 (not-green). Post-#399 the leaf inspects stdout, derives 'none' from the parseable empty-array payload → `ci_is_green` compares 'none' != 'green' → rc 1 (not-green). old=1/new=1 — genuine parity, NO decision change for this class (unlike rc_nonzero_valid_json_success which is the ONE documented decision change). This fixture certifies the real-GitHub zero-checks path is exercised end-to-end (online-review r1 blocking finding on PR #410).
